_comment: cbom-js detection registry. Every crypto asset, pattern, and rule lives here. Add a new library by adding an entry — no code changes needed.

createCipher.notes: createCipher is deprecated — use createCipheriv instead

createDecipher.notes: createDecipher is deprecated — use createDecipheriv instead

jsonwebtoken.description: JSON Web Token signing and verification

hash.notes: bcrypt — secure adaptive password hashing KDF

hashSync.notes: bcrypt — secure adaptive password hashing KDF

argon2.description: Argon2 password hashing (winner of Password Hashing Competition)

hash.notes: Argon2 — memory-hard KDF, recommended for new password hashing

tweetnacl.description: Port of TweetNaCl — public-domain crypto library

libsodium-wrappers.description: libsodium JS wrapper — modern crypto primitives

@noble/hashes.description: Audited and minimal JS implementations of hash functions

@noble/curves.description: Audited elliptic curves — P256, P384, secp256k1, Ed25519

JSEncrypt.notes: JSEncrypt — RSA PKCS#1v1.5, consider using OAEP padding instead

ModeOfOperation.ecb.notes: ECB mode — does not provide semantic security, leaks data patterns

sha.js.description: SHA hash functions in pure JavaScript

v1.notes: uuid v1 is time-based — not suitable for security-sensitive identifiers

Strategy.notes: Local strategy — verify password hashing implementation separately

algorithms._comment: Algorithm metadata — severity, quantum safety, CWEs, primitive, cryptoFunctions, classicalSecurityLevel, OID. Every field used by cbomGenerator. No hardcoding needed in code.

ED25519.notes: Ed25519 — quantum vulnerable but strong classically

EVP-KDF.notes: EVP KDF is an OpenSSL legacy KDF — prefer PBKDF2, scrypt, or Argon2

UUID-V1.notes: UUID v1 is time-based — predictable, not suitable for secrets

NONE.notes: JWT algorithm=none disables signature verification entirely

DYNAMIC-ALGO.notes: Algorithm value is dynamic — could not be resolved statically

hardcodedPatterns._comment: Variable name patterns that suggest a hardcoded secret. Tool flags when these variables are assigned string literals.

insecureRandomPatterns._comment: Patterns for detecting non-cryptographic randomness used in security contexts

patterns.notes: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure. Use crypto.randomBytes() instead
Date.now() is predictable — must not be used as cryptographic entropy

tlsPatterns._comment: TLS/HTTPS property names and values to detect in object configurations
